So it's extremely exciting and I'm going to break down each piece of it so that you understand what you're going to be building. So the key ingredient to this is the fact that we're simply providing more value to the end user. And so what I mean by that is that we now are providing them with a superior one-on-one experience. And so having a one-on-one experience nutritionally and then from an accountability standpoint allows us to anchor our pricing with personal training. In addition to that, people are less likely to churn out because it's based on a relationship and not based on a membership and they honestly get significantly better results. Now I refer to one-on-one nutrition and one-on-one accountability as hybrid but don't let that mess with your brain. It's simply nomenclature. It doesn't matter. It's just marketing. And so we are going to go over what the fulfillment looks like on top of your large group so that you can have this higher price point. But in a nutshell, from a one-on-one accountability standpoint, we're simply going to be texting our customers five days a week, which is just Monday through Friday. You're going to text them to check in how they're doing and throughout the week we check on various different things. So rather than having one extensive feedback survey, we basically ask one of the fact that it's a regular feedback cadence allows you to have a regular communication and more importantly to Suzy and making sure that she's following the plan and most importantly that she actually gets her butt in the gym. From a nutrition standpoint, we're meeting one of their progress in their food so that we can make any tweaks to the program that we have to or just to commend them, give them a high five and keep them on the track. Over time, that doesn't need to be one-on-one for this one. It can be in a small group setting or it can be done via Zoom. It depends on the customer. But when you start out, it's good to just do one-on-one nutrition concentrations every 14 days. It takes 15 minutes. And then texting them every morning. You're going to be able to delegate a lot of these responsibilities to your trainers and by extension they're going to be able to get paid a little bit more money so they're happy with it and you're going to retain more customers and be able to charge significantly more money. Below that you have semi-privates plus hybrid or semi-privates only. And so the difference here is slightly more per week. Semi-privates is just the training component which I'd recommend you do one on four or a hybrid kind of building on top of that. Now this is going to be your bread and butter. This is going to be especially for a large group training facility and you want to have minimal operational complexity which is what I recommend is you're going to be doing large group plus hybrid. This is what 85, 95% of your cost that we lead in the diagnostic selling process. Now if some reason someone doesn't want to be working out at the facility, they're too far away, the times don't work, etc. Then you have a perfect candidate for hybrid only which means that they're going to get one on one coaching for nutrition and accountability and then you give them exercise recommendations. Now exercise doesn't mean you need programming because Susie doesn't usually need programming. She just walks every day her life will change in addition to keeping her food and actually sticking with it. Now the very lowest thing that we offer here is group training only. The reason for that is because group training alone is simply not valued very much in the marketplace and it is something that is going to continually be driven down with price competition and more big players entering the marketplace and slashing prices. And so simply differentiating based on your community is not going to be enough. That is why we offer the one-on-one accountability and nutrition on top of the service so that we can decommodiatize, anchor with personal training and create a superior experience and ultimately a better outcome. The fact that they value this more is the reason. And just as a caveat to all of this, your fulfillment is going to need to change to three days per week going forward. If you haven't limited that's fine for now but every person that you are now selling in from the point you're at gym launch onwards, you're going to sell out three times a week. You will not change, like it will not change your close rates whatsoever if anything increases your close rates because they value the sessions more. And most people prefer to work less to achieve a result, not more. It also doubles the capacity of your facility which is why we do it. Now I want to illustrate the importance of what I just talked about from a pricing standpoint with a story that I've used over and over again that I think will break the belief around this. So if right now you're a normal gym, not like I'm not saying you're a normal gym, but if we took this gym example and say you had 100 EFTs, oops wrong one, and $100 a month. That means you're making $10,000 per month and let's say you have $8,000 a month in overhead here and you're making $2,000 a month in profit. That means you're running a 20% margin. Fantastic. Average gyms run less than that but let's just say that's what you're running in this hypothetical example. A genie comes to you and says I can 2x the number of customers you have, which is this guy, I can 2x how long they stay, this guy, or I can doubly same, which is this guy. The question I always ask people who are new is which one would you take? Think to yourself which one you take and I'm going to explain how each of these net out. So with twice as many customers you now would have 200 EFTs and that means that the amount of money that you would make would be 20K on your top. Your cost to fulfill usually stays about the same as you go up because you have more customers, you have more fulfillment, etc. And so that means that you'd have about 16K in cost, which means you net 4K. So you doubled your take home. Cool. Your profit margin is still the same but you doubled your absolute profit. Great. Lifetime value works differently. You still have your same number of EFTs but they're staying twice as long. So what ends up happening is that you're still making your same 10K but your cost goes down because you don't have to market as often to fill the bucket back up. And so your cost would grow from 8K to 6K and then you end up at $4,000 per month. But now you have a 40% margin business, which is why retention is so important from a profit standpoint. All right? Now, because acquiring a customer is the most expensive thing you do in a business. Now, the third thing is price. This is the third lever that you can use. Now let's take the same example. We have 100 EFTs but now we have $200 a month. Our cost of fulfillment is still the same because we have 100 customers but we're going to say that we're doing it and making it realistic with our existing adding on our nutrition and accountability. So what that's going to add is it's going to add an extra $2,000 a month income. And so we're going to go from 8K to 10K in cost. So 10K is top, 8K. This is where I'm following along with these three numbers. So our profit here is 10K. Now when that genie asks you and you have a 50% margin here, when that genie asks you which of these things would you like to double, what would you say? Price. Because if you're running a 15% margin business and you increase your prices, you double your profit. So if you go from $100 to $115 per month, you doubled the profit if you have a 15% margin business. That is why pricing is so important. So what are the requirements to hit the $1 million run rate, Jim? This is the requirement. This is what is required for you to do. You need to make 20 sales a month. That's one a day during the weekdays. And that you can even mess up four days. So if you have 24 weekdays in a month, you can still mess up on four of them. The second requirement is that you are average. The average that we have right now hits $850 up front in cash payments. That is not contract value. That is what they receive on the first day from the person walking in the door. On top of that, you'll follow the diagnostic selling system and you'll have $99 a week of recurring that is going to be added to their EFT that begins immediately. You're going to take this $850 up front and you're going to spread a discount across the duration of the time that they're here. So if they're here for 36 weeks, you take $850 divided by 36 and you get $23 off the top which brings you down to $78 a week that they actually be billing for. Still $99 a week, you just are scraping some of that to the front so that you can capture more upfront cash which increases their commitment, allows you to spend more money on advertising, allows you to pay commissions out more easily, etc. If you hit those 20 sales a month and you have 10% nutrition which is horrible. I'm using this because I want to post gyms that come to us with group training are averaging 10% month over month attrition which is not good. If you have 10% month over month attrition then and you're doing this in a one on one setting when you're messaging people every day, it's basically not realistic. It's not going to happen. You're going to be significantly lower than this. You're doing 10% with group training where you have no contact compared to where you're going to be messaging people every day providing additional value and that number is going to go down. If you started at zero and you had 10% attrition, it would take you about 14 months. If you have 5% attrition, it would take you 10 months to hit that. That's if you started today with zero. No money in your gym, no clients, no nothing. I think going from zero to a million dollar business in 10 months with a proven model is a great idea. Many things can change that lever. If for some reason or 40, that number cuts in half, you go from 10 to 5 months. That's how from zero to seven figure run rates in a few months. That's also because not everyone's starting at zero. You can also ascend your existing customers to higher levels of service and that will immediately be able to, you can always ask your coach about that. There are two ways that we're going to be driving traffic into your gym. I get the question a lot like, which one should I do? The answer is yes, both. Now, they both have costs. If you're running paid ads, you're trading money so that you can get leads. If you have 100 leads a month, which is the requirement in order to hit the million dollar gym based on what I just said, you would have 60 to 70 schedule per 100 leads per month. You'd have 35 shows and you'd have 20 sales at 850. You could just do that and do none of this and hit your seven figures. If you said, I want to trade time instead and you went the organic route, which means you make posts, you make like it and comment it and view your stories and sign up and get added to your group. All those new people get added to your free group. All of those things are places where you'll message people, get them on the typically for cold outreach. It takes 25 outreach attempts to get five responses and one sale. Those are the numbers. For 20 sales, you multiply that by 20 in order to get there. If you're multiplying by 20, it means you have 500 outreaches per month. That sounds like a lot, but if you're breaking it up per day, if you shoot for 25 a day, you should be fine. The best guys do 50 or 75 a day and they crush it and make two to three sales a day doing this. Now, which one should you do? The answer is both. The answer is both because why would you not want to have more people? If you can do both, then you're going to get there in half the time. These are the two ways that you can generate leads into your business to feed this machine. From a promotional standpoint, what we're going to be marketing overall to build our seven figure gym has nothing to do with a specific play. Any of these things work. Seven day, 14 day, 28 day, it doesn't matter. Three concepts of a promotion are going to be duration, benefit, power. It would be the 21 day lean mom accelerator, transformation, blueprint, whatever. It could be the six week little black dress challenge. This is how you make your promotions. It doesn't matter what promotion you run. You can run all of them at the same time because they're all going to funnel into a single selling system, which is going to be the diagnostic sale. All of these promotions funnel into the diagnostic selling process, which is the single best selling process that I know of for fitness. It's what we have. Everything is based on this. It's an amazing selling system. I'm excited for you to get into it. Like I said earlier, 90% of your customers are going to be taking the $99 per week, which is going to be large group training and one-on-one nutrition and accountability. That is how we're going to get the highest prices and we're going to provide the most value to the marketplace. They're great at promoting, they're great at selling, they're very disorganized, they're very energetic, but they are really scatterbrained in general. We tend to all be the same that way. It takes a more disciplined entrepreneur, a more skilled entrepreneur, to typically run a bigger business. Now, the beauty of this business model is that instead of having a retention manager, an operations manager, a challenge manager, which is the person who manages all your front-end pieces, those roles are eliminated. We're assuming that they're not going to be as motivated as us. They're going to be doing some sessions, etc., 75 full-time. Now, they're going to be able to make an additional $50 on nutrition and accountability. They're going to be able to make an extra $36,000 a year, plus whatever they're making hourly on the sessions that they're teaching, which means that your coaches are going to be able to make about $50,000 a year, which is great. You can retain great coaches for $50,000 a year. Because if you remember, we do three days a week for our workouts, which means if we have 182 people, it means that we have 90 and 90. Monday, Wednesday, Friday, we have 90. Which means that you only have to have four or five sessions a day, which keeps your overhead very low. It makes it very easy to operate this business.
